explanation: This LTL property states that globally, if the autopilot is in the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY) and the pilot is not in control (!standby), then eventually the autopilot will transition to the TRANSITION state (state_is_TRANSITION) for at least one step. This captures the requirement that the autopilot should transition to TRANSITION from STANDBY when the pilot relinquishes control.

The LTL property states that it is always the case that if the autopilot is in STANDBY state and the pilot is not in control(not standby), then eventually the autopilot will transition to TRANSITION state for a period of time or stay in TRANSITION state at any point.

The LTL property states that globally, if the autopilot is in STANDBY, and the pilot is not in control, then the autopilot eventually transitions to TRANSITION within a certain time frame. This is captured using G (state_is_STANDBY & !standby -> F[0,N_DURATION] state_is_TRANSITION) .
